# Device Alarm Notifications

> Process hardware alarm codes embedded in device position transmissions, generate individual alert events per alarm type (SOS, tamper, vibration, accident, jamming, etc.), and route notifications to...

**Category:** Notification · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · alarm · sos · panic · tamper · safety · fleet · alert

## What this does

Process hardware alarm codes embedded in device position transmissions, generate individual alert events per alarm type (SOS, tamper, vibration, accident, jamming, etc.), and route notifications to...

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **alarm** *(text, optional)* — Comma-separated list of active alarm codes in the position (e.g. sos, tampering, vibration)
- **alarm_type** *(select, optional)* — Individual alarm type. Supported values: sos, tampering, removing, vibration, movement, low_batte...
- **suppress_duplicates** *(boolean, optional)* — When true, only new alarm codes (not in the previous position) generate events; prevents repeated...

## What must be true

- **rule_1:** A position may carry multiple simultaneous alarm codes; each alarm code generates a separate alert event
- **rule_2:** If suppress_duplicates is enabled, an alarm code that was already present in the previous position does not generate a new event; only newly appearing codes trigger alerts
- **rule_3:** Each alarm event is linked to the position where it was detected, preserving exact coordinates and time
- **rule_4:** Alarm events can be routed to users via any configured notification channel (push, email, SMS, webhook)
- **rule_5:** Alarm codes are device and protocol specific; the platform normalises known codes to standard identifiers

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **No Alarm Code** — when position does not carry any alarm attribute, then No alarm event generated for this position.
- **Duplicate Alarm Suppressed** — when alarm code is present in both the current and previous position; suppress_duplicates eq true, then Duplicate alarm suppressed; no redundant alert generated.
- **Alarm Event Generated** — when position carries one or more alarm codes in the alarm attribute; suppress_duplicates is false OR the alarm code was not present in the previous position, then Alarm event stored; notification handlers dispatch alerts to subscribed users.

## Errors it can return

- `ALARM_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced in the position record does not exist

## Connects to

- **gps-position-ingestion** *(required)* — Alarm codes arrive as position attributes during ingestion
- **device-status-tracking** *(recommended)* — SOS and offline alarms may indicate the same emergency condition
- **fleet-device-sharing** *(recommended)* — Only users with access to the device receive alarm notifications
